sourceMovie.title: Casper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Casper works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hocus Pocus (1993) operates in the same fantasy as Casper, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hocus Pocus 2 (2022) belongs to the same conversation as Casper — both anchor their fantasy in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: IF
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Casper's fantasy stayed with you, IF (2024) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Orion and the Dark
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Casper excels in fantasy, Orion and the Dark (2024) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: A Babysitter's Guide to Monster Hunting
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Babysitter's Guide to Monster Hunting (2020) is the natural next step after Casper: same fantasy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Scooby-Doo! The Mystery Begins
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Casper who respond to its fantasy will find Scooby-Doo! The Mystery Begins (2009) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Curse of Bridge Hollow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Casper fans keep landing on The Curse of Bridge Hollow (2022) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Upside-Down Magic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Upside-Down Magic draws from the same fantasy well as Casper, channeling family in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2020 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Scooby-Doo! and the Curse of the 13th Ghost
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Scooby-Doo! and the Curse of the 13th Ghost (2019) captures the comedy appeal of Casper without simply imitating it. The family streak running through both films is what puts Scooby-Doo! and the Curse of the 13th Ghost at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Happy Halloween, Scooby-Doo!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy appeal of Casper — that dark edge, the family — translates cleanly into Happy Halloween, Scooby-Doo! (2020). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Casper?
faq.answer: Try Hocus Pocus, Hocus Pocus 2, IF—each shares DNA with Casper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Casper?
faq.answer: Casper (1995) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Casper part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Casper Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
